companies.name: Qualcomm
companies.relevance: Mobile SoC, RF front-end, modem supply chain

key_segments.name: Automotive
key_segments.note: Record quarter; on track for $6B+ annualized run rate by end of FY2026

key_segments.name: IoT
key_segments.note: PC and edge AI devices driving growth

key_segments.name: Handsets (QCT)
key_segments.note: Apple modem loss largely priced in; diversification offsetting

forward_signals: Custom AI silicon shipping to leading hyperscaler data center client later in 2026
OpenAI collaboration announced — AI-focused smartphone chip targeting 2028 device
Agentic smartphone tier emerging (ZTE Nubia ByteDance, Xiaomi frameworks)
Memory shortage creating near-term mobile headwinds across the industry
companies.analyst_take: Post-Apple modem reset largely absorbed. Diversification into auto and AI data center is the new growth story. Watch hyperscaler silicon ramp in H2 2026.

companies.name: Apple
companies.relevance: End-market demand signal; A-series chip architecture; component sourcing

key_segments.name: iPhone
key_segments.note: March quarter record; iPhone 17 family strongest lineup in Apple history; market share gains confirmed

key_segments.name: Services
key_segments.note: All-time high; double-digit growth in developed + emerging markets

key_segments.name: Other Products
key_segments.note: Mac and iPad stable; Vision Pro volume still minimal

forward_signals: Raised Q3 2026 guidance above Wall Street consensus
AI features cited as upgrade driver — Apple Intelligence pulling consumers to iPhone 17
A19 chip sourced from TSMC 3nm — supply tightening flagged
companies.analyst_take: Record quarter across the board. iPhone 17 cycle stronger than iPhone 15/16. Services becoming dominant profit driver. Key watchpoint: A19 TSMC supply constraints into H2 2026.

companies.name: TSMC
companies.relevance: Primary foundry for Apple A-series, Qualcomm Snapdragon, Nvidia; leading-edge node availability

key_segments.name: Advanced Nodes (7nm+)
key_segments.note: 3nm alone = 25% of wafer revenue — AI chip demand primary driver

key_segments.name: 2nm (N2)
key_segments.note: On track; N2P and N2X variants in development for 2027+

key_segments.name: 3nm (N3)
key_segments.note: Market demand ~240-250k wfpm — supply still constrained

forward_signals: Additional 3nm-capable fab announced — capex toward high end of $52-56B range
CoWoS advanced packaging still constrained — key bottleneck for AI GPU supply
Arizona fab (2nm) on track for 2026 production start
AI demand described as 'insatiable' — data center customers pulling all available capacity
Rare decision to break internal capacity rules to meet AI chip demand
companies.analyst_take: Best-in-class margins at scale. The real story is the capacity constraint decision — TSMC breaking its own rules to meet AI demand signals how extreme the supply shortage is. 2nm ramp in H2 is the key event to watch.
